build_from.reason: This is the exact extension point the epic's Proposed Solution item 1 names. It already defines FormatMarkdown/FormatJSON/FormatChecklist as string constants, a ValidFormat(s string) bool guard, a Formats() string for error messages, and a Render(w io.Writer, findings []reconcile.JSONFinding, format string) error dispatcher that switches on format. A FormatSarif constant plus a renderSarif(w, findings) function slots into the same switch with zero disruption to existing formats.
build_from.suggested_approach: Add `FormatSarif = "sarif"` alongside the existing Format* constants, add it to ValidFormat's switch and Formats()'s output, add a `case FormatSarif: return renderSarif(w, findings)` arm to Render(), and implement renderSarif in a new internal/report/sarif.go file following the renderJSON/renderChecklist pattern already in render.go (indented json.Marshal of a hand-built SARIF struct tree).

semantic_matches.relevance: The format dispatcher — exact extension point for a new FormatSarif case.
Existing format constant block; FormatSarif = "sarif" belongs here.
Format allow-list the CLI and MCP handler check before any I/O — must recognize "sarif".
Registers the --format flag (default "md") on `atcr report` — the only command that should gain sarif, per the epic's already-resolved refinement.

Validates --format via report.ValidFormat then routes to report.Render() in its default case — once FormatSarif is added to render.go, no change is needed here beyond the --format usage string, and the --disagreements combination should be rejected for sarif the same way it already is for checklist, via the existing default: usageError branch at report.go's disagreements switch.

MCP report handler validates the format via report.ValidFormat and routes non-markdown formats through report.Render. Once FormatSarif is recognized, MCP clients receive SARIF output automatically; parity with the CLI should be verified.

The struct every render function consumes: Severity, File, Line, Problem, Fix, Category, EstMinutes, Evidence, Reviewers, Confidence, Disagreement, Verification, PathValid, PathWarning, PathSuggestion. SARIF's result.locations[].physicalLocation maps from File+Line; result.message.text maps from Problem (or Problem+Fix); result.ruleId can derive from Category.

The single canonical severity rubric (CRITICAL=4, HIGH=3, MEDIUM=2, LOW=1 — see reconcile/merge.go:11-14 for the Sev* string constants) that every other severity-consuming package (report, fanout, ghaction) already reuses via NormalizeSeverity(). The SARIF formatter's CRITICAL/HIGH→error, MEDIUM→warning, LOW→note mapping must key off NormalizeSeverity(f.Severity), not redefine its own rubric — internal/fanout/postprocess.go:14 shows what happens when a severity rubric is redefined locally (flagged as TD-0052, an existing duplication bug); the new SARIF path should not add a fourth copy.

Existing convention for turning a JSONFinding into a FILE:LINE anchor string, omitting the line when it is unknown (0). SARIF's physicalLocation.region.startLine has no "unknown" representation, so the formatter needs an explicit decision for Line<=0 findings (e.g. omit the region entirely, matching SARIF's own artifactLocation-only-no-region allowance) rather than emitting a fabricated line 0.

An existing internal type for representing a file:line anchor tied to diff grounding; useful precedent for how the codebase already models a validated file/line pair, though it is unexported and specific to the justification/grounding gate rather than reusable directly by the report layer.

Existing CI docs already establish the atcr review && atcr reconcile two-step pattern this epic's Proposed Solution item 3 explicitly says to follow, plus a 'Maintained PR Action' subsection demonstrating the doc structure (fenced YAML step, link to a deeper doc) that a new 'Centralized Security Surfaces (SARIF)' subsection should mirror.

Documents the existing composite Action (action.yml) that already posts a PR check + inline comments via direct API calls — the epic's Out of Scope section correctly excludes this flow; the new SARIF doc section must not duplicate or contradict it, only cover the separate GH Advanced Security Code Scanning tab / GitLab SAST widget surfaces.
The existing direct-API PR-comment command this epic is explicitly not touching (Out of Scope) — confirms no overlap risk with the SARIF export path.

A second, independently-defined severity comparison site (uses reclib.SeverityRank correctly, but is cited by TD-0052 alongside other sites as an example of rubric duplication risk) — cautionary precedent, not a file this epic touches.

Existing diff-parsing dependency used elsewhere (internal/payload) for git-diff-aware line anchoring; not needed by the SARIF formatter itself since JSONFinding.File/Line are already resolved by the time report renders them, but confirms the repo has diff-anchoring infrastructure if line validation is ever needed at the SARIF layer.
Existing golden-file testing pattern for report.Render() output; a new SARIF golden case should be added to goldenCases and a matching internal/report/testdata/report.sarif.json fixture committed.

Existing golden-file fixture pattern for report.Render() output; a new testdata/golden/report.sarif (or internal/report/testdata/report.sarif.json) fixture should follow the same convention for the new format's tests.

existing_patterns.name: Format constant + dispatcher switch
existing_patterns.description: internal/report/render.go defines string constants for each supported format (FormatMarkdown = "md", etc.), a ValidFormat() allow-list, a Formats() error-message helper, and a Render() function that switches on the format string to call a dedicated renderX function.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Adding FormatSarif and its renderSarif function — this is the exact shape the epic's Proposed Solution item 1 asks for.

existing_patterns.name: Canonical severity rubric via reconcile.SeverityRank/NormalizeSeverity
existing_patterns.description: Severity strings are normalized case/whitespace-insensitively via reconcile.NormalizeSeverity and ranked via reconcile.SeverityRank (CRITICAL=4, HIGH=3, MEDIUM=2, LOW=1). Every consumer (report, ghaction, fanout) keys off this shared rubric rather than redefining its own.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Mapping ATCR severities to SARIF levels (result.level: error/warning/note/none) — the mapping function should take reconcile.NormalizeSeverity(f.Severity) as input, not re-parse the raw string.

existing_patterns.name: CLI format validation before I/O
existing_patterns.description: cmd/atcr/report.go validates --format against report.ValidFormat() before any file reads or writes, returning a usageError (exit 2) on an unrecognized value.
existing_patterns.follow_for: No new validation code needed for sarif — it's covered automatically once FormatSarif is added to ValidFormat(). Update the flag help text to list sarif.

existing_patterns.name: MCP report handler format validation
existing_patterns.description: internal/mcp/handlers.go:handleReport defensively re-validates the format through report.ValidFormat() and routes non-markdown output through report.Render(), so SARIF is available to MCP clients as soon as the report package supports it.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Verify MCP/CLI parity after adding FormatSarif — no code change required, but an integration test should exercise the SARIF path through the MCP handler.

existing_patterns.name: Golden-file tests per format
existing_patterns.description: internal/report/render_test.go uses a goldenCases table that renders sample() for each format and compares byte-for-byte against internal/report/testdata/*. Run `go test ./internal/report -update` to regenerate after an intentional renderer change.
existing_patterns.follow_for: Add a SARIF entry to goldenCases and a corresponding internal/report/testdata/report.sarif.json fixture.

existing_patterns.name: Documentation subsection per output/integration surface
existing_patterns.description: docs/ci-integration.md uses one subsection per integration surface (exit-code gating, then a 'Maintained PR Action' subsection with a fenced YAML snippet and a link to docs/github-action.md for depth).
existing_patterns.follow_for: The epic's AC4 CI-integration doc example — add a new subsection (or a new docs/sarif-integration.md linked from ci-integration.md) with a GitHub Actions snippet piping atcr report --format=sarif to codeql-action/upload-sarif, and the GitLab CI SAST-widget equivalent.

Wires the --format flag and routes to report.Render(); the --format usage string in the flag help text ("output format: md, json, or checklist") also needs updating to mention sarif.
Defines JSONFinding, the struct every render function consumes.
Canonical severity rubric (SeverityRank, NormalizeSeverity) to reuse for the SARIF level mapping.
Shows the existing FILE:LINE anchor convention (omit line when 0) that the new SARIF physicalLocation/region construction should mirror.
MCP report handler also consumes report.ValidFormat/report.Render; SARIF is automatically available but should be parity-checked against the CLI.
Existing CI docs page; the epic's AC4 doc example belongs here or in a new file linked from here, following its established subsection structure.
Documents the already-shipped, out-of-scope direct-API PR-comment Action — read for boundary awareness only, not modified.
Existing golden-file and behavioral test pattern for report.Render(); the new SARIF tests should extend this file or mirror its conventions.
Golden-file directory for report output; a new report.sarif.json fixture belongs here.
Documents the hand-rolled-struct default and the optional owenrumney/go-sarif/v2 alternative, plus the jsonschema-go validation approach for tests.
Already declares github.com/google/jsonschema-go v0.4.3, which tests can use to validate SARIF output without adding a new dependency.

integration_points.description: Add `case FormatSarif: return renderSarif(w, findings)` to the existing format switch.
Add FormatSarif to the recognized-format switch so `atcr report --format=sarif` and the MCP report handler pass validation.
Add FormatSarif to the comma-joined list used in usage/error messages.
Update the --format flag's help text to list sarif alongside md/json/checklist.
Non-markdown formats route through report.Render; once FormatSarif is recognized, MCP clients get SARIF output automatically. Verify parity with CLI.

integration_gaps.description: No SARIF formatter exists yet. Must create a hand-rolled SARIF 2.1.0 struct tree (run.tool.driver.{name,rules}, run.results[].{ruleId,level,message,locations}) with severity-to-level mapping via reconcile.NormalizeSeverity and Line<=0 region omission.

No SARIF-specific unit tests or golden fixture exist. Must add table-driven tests covering severity mapping (CRITICAL/HIGH→error, MEDIUM→warning, LOW→note), line anchoring including Line<=0, and SARIF-JSON schema shape; optionally validate against the SARIF 2.1.0 schema using the already-vendored google/jsonschema-go.

No SARIF upload examples exist. Must add subsections showing how to pipe `atcr review && atcr reconcile && atcr report --format=sarif` to GitHub's codeql-action/upload-sarif and the GitLab CI SAST-widget equivalent.

MCP report handler and CLI both consume report.ValidFormat/report.Render, but only the CLI has a user-visible help string to update. Add an MCP-level test or manual check that `format=sarif` returns valid SARIF JSON through the MCP tool.

reusable_components.name: report.Render dispatch pattern
reusable_components.usage_example: renderSarif(w io.Writer, findings []reconcile.JSONFinding) error, following the exact signature and json.MarshalIndent style of renderJSON.

reusable_components.name: Golden-file test harness
reusable_components.usage_example: Add {"sarif", FormatSarif, "report.sarif.json"} to goldenCases and commit the generated internal/report/testdata/report.sarif.json fixture.

architecture_notes: internal/report is the correct and only render-layer package to touch — it already owns the FormatMarkdown/FormatJSON/FormatChecklist pattern, so SARIF is a fourth sibling format, not a new subsystem.

internal/report imports the internal/reconcile package (aliased) for JSONFinding, and the top-level reconcile package (aliased reclib) for the severity rubric and Verdict constants — the new sarif.go file should follow the same two-import convention already established in render.go.

cmd/atcr/report.go's runReport() already fully generalizes over `format` via report.ValidFormat/report.Render for the non-disagreements path — no new command wiring beyond the format constant and help text is needed, confirming the epic's own refinement finding that atcr report (not atcr review/reconcile) is the correct CLI target.

internal/mcp/handlers.go:handleReport also validates against report.ValidFormat and routes non-markdown output through report.Render, so SARIF is automatically available over MCP once the report package supports it. Parity should be verified with a test.

TD-0052 (existing technical debt) flags that the severity rubric is independently redefined in multiple places; the SARIF formatter must consume reconcile.NormalizeSeverity/SeverityRank rather than adding a fifth redefinition.

SARIF's region.startLine is 1-based and has no defined behavior for line 0 — JSONFinding.Line can be 0 for file-level findings (see internal/ghaction/render.go's location() helper, which already special-cases Line<=0). The SARIF formatter needs an equivalent decision: omit the region block when Line<=0 rather than emitting startLine:0.

The project already depends on github.com/google/jsonschema-go (go.mod) for schema inference in the MCP layer; tests can reuse it to validate renderSarif output against the official SARIF 2.1.0 JSON schema without adding a new runtime dependency.

The default implementation approach is a hand-rolled struct tree (zero new runtime deps), per package-recommendations.md. owenrumney/go-sarif/v2 is reserved for a future epic if the SARIF surface grows beyond results[] + basic locations.

files_to_modify.reason: Add FormatSarif constant, extend ValidFormat/Formats/Render to recognize it.
Update the --format flag's help text to mention sarif.
Add a CI-integration example/subsection for the SARIF path (GitHub Code Scanning upload, GitLab SAST widget), per AC4.

files_to_create.purpose: renderSarif(w io.Writer, findings []reconcile.JSONFinding) error — maps JSONFinding to the SARIF 2.1.0 JSON schema (run, tool.driver, results[], physicalLocation, level mapping via reconcile severity rubric).
files_to_create.based_on: internal/report/render.go's renderJSON (indented json.MarshalIndent over a struct tree)

files_to_create.based_on: existing internal/report/render_test.go golden-case and table-driven patterns

files_to_create.purpose: Golden-file fixture for renderSarif output, following the existing internal/report/testdata/report.md convention.
